message:
chore(fp-io): scope inspect import into its single use-site (br-m8vu / fd90.46)

Self-review of fd90.38's SqlInspector noted that the test-mod use
super{...} block imported inspect (the free-fn convenience
constructor) at module level, but the only usage is inside a
#[cfg(feature = "sql-sqlite")] test. When that feature is off,
the import goes unused and rustc warns.

Move the inspect import into the cfg-gated test scope itself so
the import lives exactly where it's needed.

Note: a broader fd90.46 attempt to gate make_sql_test_conn on
sql-sqlite was reverted — it surfaced 60+ pre-existing SQL tests
that reference make_sql_test_conn unconditionally and would all
need cfg gates. That sweeping change is out of scope for this
slice; it's a pre-existing limitation of --no-default-features
test builds (lib build is fine after fd90.45). The lib clippy
under --no-default-features stays clean; default-features tests
all pass (414).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
